body.p: Clicking on Search in the left stripe opens the Search in Task page that allows you to search for specific content in all files attached to the task. If a file is open in the visual editor, clicking on Search opens the page in a side-view to the left of the editor. The page has two tabs: In content and In reviews. The search field is located inside each tab.

section.title: In content
section.p: In the In content tab, enter the text you want to search for in the search field. The search looks for matches in the text of all task files. See for advanced options. A short reference is also available in the UI via the help icon next to the search field.
You can click on a result to open that file in the visual editor (the Search in task page becomes a side-view located to the left of the editor).

section.title: In reviews
section.p: In the In reviews tab, you can search for text within review comments and other review items. The search field works the same as in In content. Next to the search field, the Filters button opens a pop-up where you can set values for the following filters:

ul.li: Type – Kind of review item (for example, comment or highlight). Default: All.
Status – Open or Done. Default: All.
Author – User who wrote the review. Default: Anyone.

Mentioned user – Restricts results to review items that contain an @-mention of the selected user (not just any occurrence of the name in text). The list is built from mentions indexed in the task; new mentions appear after re-indexing. Matching is case-insensitive. Default: Anyone. The current user is listed first in the list. See for how to add mentions in comments.
Time – Time range for the review. Default: All Time.

section.p: When you have one or more filters set, they appear as chips below the search field (each chip shows the filter icon and value). The Filters button may also show a badge when filters are active. The filter pop-up opens to the right when there is enough space.

Results are ordered by date (newest first). Each result shows the date, author, document title, and relative file path. For comments, the topic text where the review was added is also shown. Click a result to open the file in the visual editor.
